content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcar Pnlcrrcns C onrnrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street. Sacramento. CA 958 12-0807
\9t6) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
May 22,2001
Richard R. Rudnansky
Meyers Nave Riback Silver & Wilson
401 Mendocino Avenue, Suite 100
Santa Rosa, CA 95401
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-075
Dear Mr. Rudnansky.
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Politicat Reform Act (the "Act").1
QrrEsTroN
. 
May Councilmember Maguire participate in a Petaluma City Council decision to
declare AT&T in default under a cable franchise agreement?
CONCLUSION
As a commission-based salesman for CRC Networks Inc., Mr. Maguire does not
have an economic interest in AT&T. Therefore, Mr. Maguire may participate in
decisions regarding AT&T's franchise agreement with the city.
FACTS
Councilmember Maguire works as a commission salesman for CRC Networks
Inc. ("CRC"). He is compensated strictly on a commission basis and has no ownership
interest in CRC. He does not receive a salary from CRC. Mr. Maguire sold internet
access and frame relay data network circuits service (phone lines to transmit data), which
were provided by AT&T Business Services (a division of AT&T) to Simpson Strong-Tie
i Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 I 0 9 - 1 8 996, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-01-075
Page No. 2
("Simpson"). Simpson pays a monthly service charge to AT&T. AT&T pays a
percentage of the monthly service charge received from Simpson to CRC. CRC pays a
percentage of the money received from AT&T to Mr. Maguire as a commission. The
amount received by Mr. Maguire within the last l2 months has exceeded $500 and has
varied from $0 to $800 per month.
It is anticipated that a decision will come before the Petaluma City Council as to
whether or not to declare AT&T in default under a cable franchise agreement with the
City of Petaluma. Councilmember Maguire wishes to participate in the discussion and
determination of the issue.
ANALYSIS
A public official may not make, participate in making, or in any way attempt to
use his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official
knows, or has reason to know, that he or she has a financial interest. (Section 87100).
The Commission has adopted a standard analysis for deciding whether an ofticial has a
disqualifying conflict of interest. (Regulation 18700, subdivisions (b)(t) - (8) )
1. Public Offrcial
The conflict-oflinterest provisions apply only to public oflicials. ($ 87100). As a
council member, Mr. Maguire is a public offrcial for the purposes of the Act. ($ 82048;
Reg. 18701).
2. Conduct Covered
The conflict-of-interest provisions cover specific conduct: making, participating
in making, or attempting to use the official's position to influence a governmental
decision. ($ 87100.) Discussing and voting on a council decision whether to declare
AT&T in default under a contract with the city constitutes making or participating in
making a governmental decision. (Reg. 18702.1(a)(t) and (3).)
3. Identifying Relevant Economic Interests
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from economic interests. ($ 87103;Reg. 18703.) Sources of income are economic
interests for public oflicials. Regulation 18703.3, subdivision (c)(3)(F), defines those
sources of commission income for a salesperson that will require disqualification:
"(F) A retail or wholesale salesperson:
(i) The person, store or other business entity which provides the
salesperson with the product or service to sell and for which the
salesperson acts as a representative in the transaction; and


File No. A-01-075
Page No. 3
(ii) The person who purchases the product or service."
Based on Regulation 18703.3, Mr. Maguire has an economic interest in (1) CRC
Networks Inc (under subdivision (i) above) and Simpson Strong-Tie (under subdivision
(ii)). Mr. Maguire could also have an economic interest in AT&T if AT&T is a parent or
subsidiary of, or is otherwise related to, CRC or Simpson Strong-Tie. (Reg. 18703.1(c),
(dxz) ) You have provided no facts indicating that AT&T and CRC, or AT&T and
Simpson, have a controlling ownership interest in each other, or that there is shared
management and control within the meaning of Regulation 18703.3, subdivision (d)(Z).
You also have provided no facts to indicate that the government decision contemplated
will have any financial effect on any of Mr. Maguire's economic interests identified
above. Accordingly, Mr. Maguire may participate in the decision regarding the AT&T
cable franchise agreement with the city.2 To the extent these facts change, we encourage
you to seek advice in the future as circumstances warrant.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely
Luisa
General
By: C. Scott T
Counsel, Legal Division
CSrjg
I:\Adviceltrs\01-075
2 This advice is applicable and confers immunity (see Section 83114) only to the extent that the
facts provided to us are correct, and that all ofthe material facts have been disclosed. (In re Oglesby,
(197s) I FPPC Ops. 71.)

sections.conclusion:
As a commission-based salesman for CRC Networks Inc., Mr. Maguire does not
have an economic interest in AT&T. Therefore, Mr. Maguire may participate in
decisions regarding AT&T's franchise agreement with the city.

embedding.qa_text:
CONCLUSION: As a commission-based salesman for CRC Networks Inc., Mr. Maguire does not
have an economic interest in AT&T. Therefore, Mr. Maguire may participate in
decisions regarding AT&T's franchise agreement with the city.
